evals.name: add-packagerating-npm-only
evals.expected_output: Detects package.json only, confirms with the user before writing, generates .github/workflows/audit-dependencies.yml with on: pull_request, permissions: pull-requests: write, and api-key: ${{ secrets.PACKAGERATING_API_KEY }}, asks about fail-on-* thresholds before writing, never commits/pushes, never asks for the raw API key value, points to packagerating.com/signup and gives the exact gh secret set command.
assertions: Detected package.json and reported it as the basis for setting up the npm action, before writing any file
Generated workflow references packagerating/audit-dependencies@v1 with api-key: ${{ secrets.PACKAGERATING_API_KEY }} and permissions: pull-requests: write
No file was staged, committed, pushed, or a PR opened
Asked (or stated it would ask) about fail-on-general/fail-on-automation/fail-on-risk thresholds before generating the workflow, not after
User was never asked to paste their API key; was given the exact gh secret set PACKAGERATING_API_KEY --repo <owner>/<repo> command and pointed to packagerating.com/signup

evals.name: setup-ci-python-only-implicit
evals.prompt: set up dependency scoring in CI for this project
evals.expected_output: Triggers despite no explicit mention of packagerating/audit-dependencies by name, detects requirements.txt only, generates .github/workflows/audit-dependencies-python.yml with the correct action reference (packagerating/audit-dependencies-python@v1), same thresholds/secret/no-commit behavior as eval 0.
assertions: Correctly triggered/handled the request despite no explicit mention of packagerating or audit-dependencies by name
Detected requirements.txt and reported it as the basis for setting up the Python action, before writing any file
Generated workflow references packagerating/audit-dependencies-python@v1 (not the npm action) with api-key: ${{ secrets.PACKAGERATING_API_KEY }} and permissions: pull-requests: write
No file was staged, committed, pushed, or a PR opened
Asked (or stated it would ask) about fail-on-* thresholds before generating the workflow
User was never asked to paste their API key; was given the exact gh secret set command and pointed to packagerating.com/signup

evals.name: integrate-dual-ecosystem-existing-ci
evals.prompt: integrate packagerating.com's dependency scoring — we already have some CI set up
evals.expected_output: Detects both package.json and requirements.txt, writes both workflow files, correctly does NOT flag the pre-existing unrelated ci.yml as a conflicting packagerating workflow (since it doesn't reference packagerating/audit-dependencies), asks about thresholds for each ecosystem.
assertions: Detected both package.json and requirements.txt and reported both before writing any files
Correctly did NOT flag the pre-existing unrelated ci.yml as a conflicting/existing packagerating workflow, since it doesn't reference packagerating/audit-dependencies

Generated separate workflow files for npm (packagerating/audit-dependencies@v1) and Python (packagerating/audit-dependencies-python@v1), each with api-key: ${{ secrets.PACKAGERATING_API_KEY }} and permissions: pull-requests: write
No file was staged, committed, pushed, or a PR opened; existing ci.yml was left untouched
Asked (or stated it would ask) about fail-on-* thresholds for the setup
User was never asked to paste their API key; was given the exact gh secret set command
